text:
🇹🇭 THAILAND — MARKET DATA & LIQUIDITY 
1. Why this matters
Indicates availability of official housing data, transparency of the property market, and reliability of liquidity indicators.
2. Official Transaction Visibility
(Official Fact)
Real estate transaction data in Thailand is recorded and published through:
Department of Lands (DOL)
Bank of Thailand (BOT)
3. What Official Data Exists
(Official Fact)
Government sources provide:
Property transfer registrations
Mortgage and lending statistics
Real estate market indicators
(Official Fact)
Bank of Thailand publishes:
Housing loan data
Property market trends and financial indicators
4. What Official Data Does NOT Exist
(Official Fact)
There is no standardized national metric for:
Average time-to-sell property
Unified liquidity ratio
5. Market Structure / Liquidity Context
(Official Fact)
Market activity is concentrated in:
Bangkok Metropolitan Region
Major tourist destinations (e.g., Phuket, Pattaya)
(Advisory Caution)
Liquidity varies significantly across:
Urban vs secondary markets
Condominium vs landed property segments
6. Why Liquidity Cannot Be Judged Only from Volume
(Advisory Caution)
Transaction volume alone is not sufficient because:
Market demand is location-dependent
No standardized time-to-sell data
Foreign ownership restrictions impact resale market
7. Advisory Caution
(Advisory Caution)
Liquidity assessment should consider:
Regional demand concentration
Property type
Foreign ownership limitations
8. What Needs Confirmation (Analytical Context)
Transaction activity in specific location
Demand for specific property type
Market conditions at time of entry/exit
9. Sources
https://www.dol.go.th
https://www.bot.or.th
10. Verification Date
07/04/2026
11. Escalation Note
Not Required –
Market data availability and limitations are clearly defined through official sources.
